name: General equity research (Buffett framework)
summary: Generic equity-research framework grounded in Warren Buffett's four pillars: (1) durable business quality — consistent ROE > 15% and pricing power; (2) capital-allocation discipline — declining or stable share count, high reinvestment ROI; (3) fortress balance sheet — debt/equity < 0.5, interest coverage > 5x, growing free cash flow; (4) valuation with margin of safety — owner earnings yield (FCF / market cap) attractive vs the 10-year Treasury, P/E reasonable for the growth profile. Used for ad-hoc drill-ins on tickers that don't fit a pre-built thematic thesis. The Synthesis agent reads this thesis and defaults bull/bear framing to the four pillars rather than a thesis-level bet.

valuation.erp_basis: Long-run S&P 500 ERP ~4.5%, rounded to 5% for the universal default. No thesis-specific tilt — ad-hoc tickers span sectors and risk profiles.
valuation.terminal_growth_basis: US real long-run GDP trend; slightly conservative (closer to Buffett's preferred 2-3% range) since this is a universal default, not a high-growth thesis.
